title: Agent Instructions: zeroserve
text: zeroserve high-performance, frontend-agnostic transport layer R ecosystem. primary goal enable zero-copy binary transport massive datasets backends (DuckDB, Arrow, sf) modern web frontends serving local HTTP server.

title: Agent Instructions: zeroserve
text: currently implementing Grand Rollout Plan (see roadmap.md context, though untracked). focus building minimal, robust transport layer without getting bogged mapping styling logic.

title: Agent Instructions: zeroserve
text: starting Phase 1: Minimal Core Transport. - Objective: Create zs_serve_arrow() zs_serve_parquet() APIs. - Reference Code: Existing prototype logic moved R_drafts/. Use files source truth previously explored implementation details, aim clean, production-ready implementation R/ directory.

title: Agent Instructions: zeroserve
text: Minimalism: Stick transport layer. Don’t re-implement mapping logic belongs mapgl geoarrowDeckglLayers. Performance: Prioritize zero-copy binary paths (Arrow, ALTREP). TDD: Follow project’s TDD workflow defined conductor/workflow.md. Interoperability: Ensure generated URLs work seamlessly geoarrowWidget.

title: Contributing to zeroserve
text: Report Bugs: find bug, please open issue GitHub minimal reproducible example. Suggest Features: welcome feature requests! Please open issue discuss ideas. Fork repository. Create new branch changes. Ensure code follows package’s style (snake_case, 80-character line limit). Add tests new functionality. Run devtools::check() locally submitting.

title: Contributing to zeroserve
text: set development environment, need R several dependencies: look forward contributions!
code: install.packages(c("devtools", "testthat", "roxygen2", "knitr", "quarto")) devtools::install_deps(dependencies = TRUE)

title: Introduction to zeroserve
text: package focuses two main methods serving data: Arrow IPC Streams: Best -memory data shared minimal overhead using shared memory. Parquet Files: Best larger--memory data data already disk, served via HTTP support range requests.

title: Introduction to zeroserve
text: serve sf object Arrow stream: serve DuckDB table streamable Parquet file:
code: library(zeroserve) library(sf) Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE # Create some sample data nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"), quiet = TRUE)  # Serve the data url <- zs_serve_arrow(nc) print(url) [1] "http://127.0.0.1:8045/stream.arrow" # Verify the data (e.g., using curl) # This simulates what a frontend would do res <- curl::curl_fetch_memory(url) cat("Fetched", length(res$content), "bytes of Arrow IPC data\n") Fetched 56656 bytes of Arrow IPC data # Clean up when done zs_stop_server() [1] TRUE library(zeroserve) library(DBI)  # Connect to DuckDB con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb())  # Load built-in data into DuckDB dbWriteTable(con, "mtcars", mtcars)  # Serve a query result url <- zs_serve_parquet(con, "SELECT * FROM mtcars", layer_id = "cars") print(url) [1] "http://127.0.0.1:8305/cars.parquet" # Verify the data # The arrow package can read directly from the URL df <- arrow::read_parquet(url) head(df) # A data frame: 6 × 11     mpg   cyl  disp    hp  drat    wt  qsec    vs    am  gear  carb * <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> 1  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.62  16.5     0     1     4     4 2  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.88  17.0     0     1     4     4 3  22.8     4   108    93  3.85  2.32  18.6     1     1     4     1 4  21.4     6   258   110  3.08  3.22  19.4     1     0     3     1 5  18.7     8   360   175  3.15  3.44  17.0     0     0     3     2 6  18.1     6   225   105  2.76  3.46  20.2     1     0     3     1 # Clean up zs_stop_server() [1] TRUE dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

title: Introduction to zeroserve
text: serve sf object Arrow stream:
code: library(zeroserve) library(sf) Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE # Create some sample data nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"), quiet = TRUE)  # Serve the data url <- zs_serve_arrow(nc) print(url) [1] "http://127.0.0.1:8045/stream.arrow" # Verify the data (e.g., using curl) # This simulates what a frontend would do res <- curl::curl_fetch_memory(url) cat("Fetched", length(res$content), "bytes of Arrow IPC data\n") Fetched 56656 bytes of Arrow IPC data # Clean up when done zs_stop_server() [1] TRUE

title: Introduction to zeroserve
text: serve DuckDB table streamable Parquet file:
code: library(zeroserve) library(DBI)  # Connect to DuckDB con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb())  # Load built-in data into DuckDB dbWriteTable(con, "mtcars", mtcars)  # Serve a query result url <- zs_serve_parquet(con, "SELECT * FROM mtcars", layer_id = "cars") print(url) [1] "http://127.0.0.1:8305/cars.parquet" # Verify the data # The arrow package can read directly from the URL df <- arrow::read_parquet(url) head(df) # A data frame: 6 × 11     mpg   cyl  disp    hp  drat    wt  qsec    vs    am  gear  carb * <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> 1  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.62  16.5     0     1     4     4 2  21       6   160   110  3.9   2.88  17.0     0     1     4     4 3  22.8     4   108    93  3.85  2.32  18.6     1     1     4     1 4  21.4     6   258   110  3.08  3.22  19.4     1     0     3     1 5  18.7     8   360   175  3.15  3.44  17.0     0     0     3     2 6  18.1     6   225   105  2.76  3.46  20.2     1     0     3     1 # Clean up zs_stop_server() [1] TRUE dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

title: Introduction to zeroserve
text: One key features zeroserve support HTTP Range requests. allows client-side applications (like using DuckDB-Wasm) fetch specific parts Parquet file need, rather downloading entire file.
code: library(zeroserve)  # Serve a file (e.g., the built-in mtcars as a temp file) temp_p <- tempfile(fileext = ".parquet") arrow::write_parquet(mtcars, temp_p) url <- zs_serve_file(temp_p)  # Fetch only the first 100 bytes using a Range header h <- curl::new_handle() curl::handle_setheaders(h, Range = "bytes=0-99") res <- curl::curl_fetch_memory(url, handle = h)  # Check the response print(res$status_code) # Should be 206 (Partial Content) [1] 206 cat("Fetched", length(res$content), "bytes\n") Fetched 100 bytes print(res$headers["content-range"]) [1] 00 # Clean up zs_stop_server() [1] TRUE

what: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving
title: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving
text: high-performance, frontend-agnostic transport layer streams large datasets directly browser use htmlwidgets, Shiny apps, custom web frontends. zeroserve leverages zero-copy Arrow IPC streams (via shared memory) HTTP Range requests (disk-based files) bypass bottlenecks JSON serialization. general-purpose, provides specialized support DuckDB, sf, Arrow objects, bridging R memory web visualizations minimal overhead.

title: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving
text: can install development version zeroserve GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("e-kotov/zeroserve")

title: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving
text: Serve sf object directly R memory local URL zero copies.
code: library(zeroserve)  # Load some spatial data nc <- sf::st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"), quiet = TRUE)  # Serve it! url <- zs_serve_arrow(nc) # [1] "http://127.0.0.1:8080/stream.arrow"

title: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving
text: Serve massive DuckDB table Parquet stream supporting HTTP Range requests.
code: library(DBI) con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb())  # ... perform massive DuckDB operations ...  # Serve the result as a streamable Parquet file url <- zs_serve_parquet(con, "SELECT * FROM massive_table") # [1] "http://127.0.0.1:8080/stream.parquet"

title: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving
text: Server: call zs_serve_* function, zeroserve spawns lightweight background R process (via callr) running httpuv server. Arrow, data shared via POSIX shared memory (using mori), allowing background server read R’s memory zero overhead. Parquet, data written temporary file, server hosts support partial content requests. Lifecycle: background server associated resources automatically cleaned R session ends package unloaded.

title: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving
